title: machaeranthera canescens var. leucanthemifolia
intro: Machaeranthera canescens var. leucanthemifolia, also known as Gray-headed False Goldenrod or White-Flowered Machaeranthera, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Machaeranthera canescens var. leucanthemifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and treat skin conditions such as wounds and minor cuts. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil to create a medicinal solution.
